alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our proposed algorithm works for all point configurations by formulating the problem in quaternions and decoupling the rotation and translation estimation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method recovers rotation and translation changes separately without using an essential matrix.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the core technical approach (separate rotation/translation recovery) but drops the strong universal claim about handling "all point configurations" and the quaternion formulation detail, making the claim weaker and narrower.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed algorithm is more robust to noise and outliers compared to some of the most commonly used algorithms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: On average, the proposed approach shows better accuracy in the presence of noise compared to several state-of-the-art algorithms.
alignments.rationale: Both assert improved performance under noise, but the publication reframes it as average accuracy gains (and omits explicit mention of outliers and the exact comparator set), a more specific/tempered formulation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Traditional essential matrix based algorithms fail when points are on critical surfaces, such as coplanar points.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not state the preprint's explicit claim that traditional essential-matrix methods fail on critical/coplanar configurations, so this limitation claim was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The implementation of our algorithm has been made available online and free for the benefit of the community.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The implementation of the proposed algorithm is available online and free for the benefit of the community.
alignments.rationale: The implementation availability claim is reiterated verbatim in the publication with no substantive change.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method has a larger execution time compared to several state-of-the-art algorithms.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit performance-cost claim (higher execution time) that did not appear in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed predictive-corrective networks can adaptively focus computation on 'surprising' frames where predictions require large corrections.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed predictive-corrective networks can adaptively focus computation on 'surprising' frames where predictions require large corrections.
alignments.rationale: The claim is carried over identically from preprint to publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The predictive-corrective networks simplify learning by requiring only 'residual-like' corrective terms to be learned over time.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The predictive-corrective networks simplify learning by requiring only 'residual-like' corrective terms to be learned over time.
alignments.rationale: The formulation and meaning of this claim are unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model naturally decorrelates an input data stream in a hierarchical fashion, producing a more reliable signal for learning at each layer of a network.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model naturally decorrelates an input data stream in a hierarchical fashion, producing a more reliable signal for learning at each layer of a network.
alignments.rationale: The claim content is the same but the publication lists a lower certainty (medium vs high), indicating a tempered confidence level.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model is competitive with the two-stream network on three challenging datasets without the need for computationally expensive optical flow.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model is competitive with the two-stream network on three challenging datasets without the need for computationally expensive optical flow.
alignments.rationale: This performance claim is repeated verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel sampler presented is faster, both empirically and theoretically, than previous Gibbs samplers for LDA.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel sampler presented is faster, both empirically and theoretically, than previous Gibbs samplers for LDA.
alignments.rationale: The performance claim is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approximation error of the novel sampler vanishes with data size, making the algorithm asymptotically exact.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approximation error of the novel sampler vanishes with data size, making the algorithm asymptotically exact.
alignments.rationale: The asymptotic-exactness claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Partially collapsed samplers can be more efficient than fully collapsed samplers, contrary to popular belief in the topic modeling literature.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Partially collapsed samplers can be more efficient than fully collapsed samplers, contrary to popular belief in the topic modeling literature.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about relative efficiency of partially vs fully collapsed samplers is preserved exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The investigation of recent Deep Learning architectures and strategies for document image classification reduces the error by more than half.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The investigation of recent Deep Learning architectures and strategies for document image classification reduces the error by more than half.
alignments.rationale: The high-level conclusion about large error reduction is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method achieves an accuracy of 91.13% for the Tobacco-3482 dataset, significantly higher than the 77.6% accuracy of earlier approaches.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method achieves an accuracy of 91.13% for the Tobacco-3482 dataset, significantly higher than the 77.6% accuracy of earlier approaches.
alignments.rationale: The numeric result and comparison are reproduced exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: For the large dataset RVL-CDIP, an accuracy of 90.97% is achieved, resulting in a relative error reduction of 11.5%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: For the large dataset RVL-CDIP, an accuracy of 90.97% is achieved, resulting in a relative error reduction of 11.5%.
alignments.rationale: This quantitative result is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper contributes by proposing transfer learning from a huge set of 400,000 document images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper investigates the impact of the amount of training data and other parameters on classification abilities.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the contribution as a broader investigation into dataset size and parameters rather than the specific contribution of transfer learning from a 400k image corpus, changing the claim's focus.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed machine learning based feature extraction scheme outperforms most existing hand crafted or deterministic feature schemes found in literature.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed feature extraction scheme based on inception models outperforms most existing hand crafted or deterministic feature schemes found in literature.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the architectural basis (inception models), clarifying the method while preserving the original performance claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model achieves an average maximum Dice of 0.85 on the DRIVE data set, outperforming the scores from the second human annotator of this data set.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model achieves an average maximum Dice of 0.85 on the DRIVE data set, surpassing the scores from the second human annotator.
alignments.rationale: The numerical result and comparison to the second annotator are repeated with equivalent wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model achieves an average maximum Dice of 0.85 and kappa of 0.84 on the STARE data set.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model achieves an average maximum Dice of 0.85 and kappa of 0.84 on the STARE data set.
alignments.rationale: This performance claim is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The feature extraction scheme can be extended to handle 3-D datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The feature extraction scheme can be extended to handle 3-D datasets.
alignments.rationale: The proposed extensibility to 3-D datasets is preserved in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed improved Saliency Network automatically delineates the contour of the glottis from HSV image sequences. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed improved Saliency Network automatically delineates the contour of the glottis from HSV image sequences. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result verbatim with same certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The additional saliency measure, Motion Saliency, improves upon the original Saliency Network by using the velocities of defined edges. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The additional saliency measure, Motion Saliency, improves upon the original Saliency Network by using the velocities of defined edges. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this contribution identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The effectiveness of the proposed approach is demonstrated in the results and analysis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The effectiveness of the proposed approach is demonstrated in the results and analysis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The demonstration claim appears unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method has potential applications for computer-aided assessment of voice pathologies and understanding voice production. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method has potential applications for computer-aided assessment of voice pathologies and understanding voice production. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding application possibilities are restated with the same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed framework for autonomous driving using deep reinforcement learning demonstrates learning of autonomous maneuvering in complex road curvatures. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Simulation results demonstrate learning of autonomous maneuvering in a scenario of complex road curvatures and simple interaction of other vehicles. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the core result but adds scenario detail (simple interactions), narrowing the situations demonstrated.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework incorporates Recurrent Neural Networks for information integration, enabling the car to handle partially observable scenarios. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed framework for autonomous driving using deep reinforcement learning incorporates Recurrent Neural Networks for information integration. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the RNN/information-integration point but omits the explicit claim about handling partially observable scenarios, reducing the claim's breadth and strength.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The integration of attention models in the framework reduces computational complexity for deployment on embedded hardware. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework integrates attention models to focus on relevant information, reducing computational complexity for deployment on embedded hardware. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this contribution with equivalent wording and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Prestack seismic data can be effectively used for seismic facies recognition. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: No publication statement affirms the general effectiveness of prestack data for facies recognition; the publication focuses on challenges and method-specific benefits instead.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A convolutional autoencoder (CAE) network is more effective than principal component analysis (PCA) for redundancy removal and valid information extraction from prestack seismic data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DCAE provides a much higher resolution than the conventional methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication asserts higher resolution versus 'conventional methods' rather than specifically PCA; this generalizes the claim beyond PCA and reframes it as resolution improvement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using conventional classification or clustering techniques on the features extracted by the CAE network allows for successful seismic facies recognition. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly claim successful facies recognition using conventional classifiers on extracted features.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method demonstrates superiority over conventional methods in seismic facies recognition. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DCAE provides a much higher resolution than the conventional methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes higher resolution and potential to highlight stratigraphy, rather than explicitly claiming overall superiority in facies recognition, which weakens and narrows the original claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Effective feature extraction becomes more challenging when transitioning from poststack trace vectors to a prestack trace matrix. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: This limitation on feature extraction complexity appears in the publication but was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The process of bagging and combining results from multiple trees produces numerous diagnostics that provide insight into class structure in high dimensions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The process of bagging and combining results from multiple trees produces numerous diagnostics that provide insight into class structure in high dimensions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result statement is repeated unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper explores various aspects to assess model complexity, individual model contributions, variable importance, and uncertainty in prediction associated with individual observations. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The article explores various aspects of models to assess model complexity, individual model contributions, variable importance, and uncertainty in prediction. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains the same set of explored aspects but presents them with higher certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ideas presented in the paper can be applied to the random forest algorithm and the projection pursuit forest, as well as to other bagged ensembles. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ideas presented can be applied to the random forest algorithm and projection pursuit forest, as well as other bagged ensembles. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The applicability claim is retained but presented with slightly lower stated certainty in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Interactive graphics built in R using the ggplot2, plotly, and shiny packages enhance the interpretability of forest classification models. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a new contribution about interactive graphics that was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analysis of Group Importance Sampling (GIS) yields several theoretical and practical consequences. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The analysis of Group Importance Sampling (GIS) yields several theoretical and practical consequences. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this conclusion unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Independent Multiple Try Metropolis schemes can be interpreted as a standard Metropolis-Hastings algorithm, following the GIS approach. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Independent Multiple Try Metropolis schemes can be interpreted as a standard Metropolis–Hastings algorithm using the GIS approach. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conceptual interpretation is restated identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Group Metropolis Sampling method produces a Markov chain of sets of weighted samples that are used to obtain a unique global estimator. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Group Metropolis Sampling method produces a Markov chain of sets of weighted samples for obtaining a unique global estimator. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The method and its estimator usage are described the same way in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Distributed Particle Metropolis-Hastings technique employs different parallel particle filters to drive an MCMC algorithm. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Distributed Particle Metropolis–Hastings technique utilizes different parallel particle filters to drive an MCMC algorithm. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this result with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel schemes are tested in various numerical experiments and compared with several benchmark Monte Carlo techniques. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel schemes are tested in various numerical experiments and compared with several benchmark Monte Carlo techniques. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Experimental evaluation and comparisons are present and unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed framework allows for learning different kinds of explanations for any black box algorithm. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed framework allows for learning different kinds of explanations for any black box algorithm. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication texts are identical in meaning and scope, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The specialized framework identifies the part of an image most responsible for a classifier decision. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The specialized framework identifies the part of an image most responsible for a classifier decision. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The claim wording and intent are the same in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method is model-agnostic and testable due to its grounding in explicit and interpretable image perturbations. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method is model-agnostic and testable due to its grounding in explicit and interpretable image perturbations. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Identical formulation and emphasis in both versions, so no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Replacing the proximal operator of the regularization in convex energy minimization algorithms with a denoising neural network serves as an implicit natural image prior. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Replacing the proximal operator of the regularization in convex energy minimization algorithms with a denoising neural network serves as an implicit natural image prior. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Exact match in claim content and emphasis between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using a fixed denoising neural network in image deconvolution and demosaicking problems yields state-of-the-art reconstruction results. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using a fixed denoising neural network in image deconvolution and demosaicking problems yields state-of-the-art reconstruction results. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result claim is unchanged in wording and meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach indicates high generalizability and reduces the need for problem-specific training. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach indicates a high generalizability and reduces the need for problem-specific training. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Same conclusion stated in both versions with only a minor stylistic change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper discusses novel results on optimization algorithms for incorporating the neural network and their relation to the noise level. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper discusses novel results on optimization algorithms for incorporating the neural network and their relation to the noise level. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Identical contribution claim in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The inclusion of a super resolution algorithm resulted in a significantly improved recognition rate over recently reported results obtained from unsupervised algorithms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The inclusion of a super resolution algorithm resulted in significant improvement in recognition rate over recently reported results obtained from unsupervised algorithms on the same dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication adds the qualification 'on the same dataset', clarifying the comparison context and thereby narrowing the scope of the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This paper aims to demonstrate the effect of one of the state-of-the-art algorithms in the field of image super resolution. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This paper aims to demonstrate the effect of one of the state-of-the-art algorithms in the field of image super resolution. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Claim is verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Various before and after 3D face alignment cases are provided using the images from the Labeled Faces in the Wild (lfw). (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Various before and after 3D face alignment cases are provided using the images from the Labeled Faces in the Wild (lfw) dataset. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Only minor wording change ('dataset' added); the substantive claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed non-convex weighted $\text{l}_p$ minimization based group sparse representation framework outperforms many state-of-the-art denoising methods such as BM3D and WNNM. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed nonconvex weighted ℓp minimization based group sparse representation framework outperforms many state-of-the-art denoising methods such as BM3D and weighted nuclear norm minimization. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Terminology differs slightly (WNNM spelled out) but the substantive claim of outperforming those methods is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The generalized soft-thresholding algorithm is adopted to solve the non-convex $\text{l}_p$ minimization problem, making the proposed scheme tractable and robust. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The generalized soft-thresholding algorithm is effective in solving the nonconvex ℓp minimization problem for image denoising. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Publication emphasizes the algorithm's effectiveness for denoising rather than the implementation detail of adoption and claims of tractability/robustness, clarifying the outcome of using the algorithm.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An adaptive patch search scheme is proposed to improve the accuracy of nonlocal similar patches selection. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An adaptive patch search scheme improves the accuracy of nonlocal similar patch selection in image denoising. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Preprint frames this as a proposed scheme; publication states it improves accuracy (a stronger demonstrated claim), so the claim is revised and the confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AMIDST Toolbox is designed for scalable probabilistic machine learning with a focus on massive streaming data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The AMIDST Toolbox is an open source Java software designed for scalable probabilistic machine learning. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Publication wording emphasizes open-source Java implementation and omits the explicit 'massive streaming data' focus present in the preprint, changing the emphasis and narrowing the claimed focus.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The toolbox supports a flexible modeling language based on probabilistic graphical models with latent variables and temporal dependencies. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AMIDST supports a flexible modelling language based on probabilistic graphical models with latent variables. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Publication restates the modelling language support; temporal dependencies are not explicitly mentioned but the core claim about probabilistic graphical models with latent variables remains, so it's a minor clarification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AMIDST enables learning from large data sets using parallel or distributed implementations of Bayesian learning algorithms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The toolbox provides parallel and distributed implementations of scalable algorithms for probabilistic inference and Bayesian parameter learning. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Publication rephrases the capability more precisely (probabilistic inference and parameter learning) while preserving the claim about parallel/distributed scalable implementations.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithms in AMIDST are based on a flexible variational message passing scheme that accommodates both discrete and continuous variables. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithms in AMIDST are based on a flexible variational message passing scheme that accommodates both discrete and continuous variables. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This algorithmic description is the same in both preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An automatic method using dilated convolutional neural networks (CNNs) was proposed for segmentation of the myocardium and blood pool in cardiovascular MR of patients with congenital heart disease.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed automatic method using dilated convolutional neural networks (CNNs) provides accurate segmentation of the myocardium and blood pool in cardiovascular MR images of patients with congenital heart disease.
alignments.rationale: The publication claim restates the same method and outcome with essentially the same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Automatic segmentation of the test scans resulted in Dice indices of 0.80±0.06 for the myocardium and 0.93±0.02 for the blood pool.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Automatic segmentation of the test scans resulted in Dice indices of 0.80±0.06 for the myocardium and 0.93±0.02 for the blood pool.
alignments.rationale: The numerical results are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The average distances to boundaries were 0.96±0.31 mm for the myocardium and 0.89±0.24 mm for the blood pool.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The average distances to boundaries were 0.96±0.31 mm for the myocardium and 0.89±0.24 mm for the blood pool.
alignments.rationale: The boundary distance measurements are identical in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Segmentation took an average of 41.5±14.7 seconds per scan.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Segmentation took an average of 41.5±14.7 seconds per scan.
alignments.rationale: The runtime result is the same in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Dilated CNNs trained on a small set of CMR images of CHD patients provide accurate segmentations despite large anatomical variability.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed automatic method using dilated convolutional neural networks (CNNs) provides accurate segmentation of the myocardium and blood pool in cardiovascular MR images of patients with congenital heart disease.
alignments.rationale: The publication omits the preprint's emphasis on training from a small dataset and explicit robustness 'despite large anatomical variability', so the claim is narrowed and somewhat tempered compared with the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Positioning data can be used to identify high density areas (hotspots) and characterize their activity over time.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We exploit positioning data to identify high-density areas (hotspots) and characterize their activity over time.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same use of positioning data to detect hotspots and characterize temporal activity.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach based on stigmergy enables the self-organization of data for adaptive analysis of urban dynamics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach based on stigmergy enables the self-organization of positioning data for adaptive analysis of urban dynamics.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes explicit that the self-organization applies to positioning data, clarifying the preprint's more general wording without changing the claim's intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analysis of taxi traces in New York City during 2015 reveals unexpected urban activity patterns.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our analysis of taxi traces in New York City during 2015 reveals unexpected urban activity patterns.
alignments.rationale: The claim content is the same but the publication presents it with higher certainty (preprint was medium, publication labeled high), indicating an amplification of confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The characterization of urban activity allows for the comparison of dynamics occurring on different days.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The characterization of urban activity allows for the comparison of dynamics occurring on different days, providing a similarity measure for clustering techniques.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the original claim and adds that this comparison yields a similarity measure useful for clustering, broadening and clarifying the application.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel method using a generative neural network can reconstruct the solid-void structure of porous media.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A method to reconstruct the solid-void structure of porous media using a generative neural network has been developed.
alignments.rationale: The publication claim mirrors the preprint contribution about using a generative network to reconstruct solid-void structure.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The adversarial learning approach for neural networks enables unsupervised learning to generate representative samples of porous media that honor their statistics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The generative adversarial networks can generate representative samples of porous media that honor their statistics.
alignments.rationale: Both statements assert that adversarial/generative networks produce representative porous media samples respecting statistical properties; wording is slightly condensed but the meaning is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GANs can reconstruct high-resolution three-dimensional images of porous media that are representative of the morphology of the training images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The trained neural network can reconstruct high-resolution three-dimensional images of porous media at different scales.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes reconstruction at different scales while the preprint emphasized representativeness of morphology; the core capability is preserved but the publication clarifies/expands the scope to multi-scale reconstruction.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The trained neural network allows for the generation of large samples of porous media while maintaining computational efficiency.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The implicit representation of the learned data distribution allows for rapid generation of multiple realizations of the pore structure.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the efficiency as rapid generation via an implicit representation; this clarifies the mechanism behind the preprint's claim about efficient large-sample generation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The implicit representation of the learned data distribution can be stored and reused to generate multiple realizations of the pore structure rapidly.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The implicit representation of the learned data distribution allows for rapid generation of multiple realizations of the pore structure.
alignments.rationale: This claim is essentially identical between preprint and publication, both describing storage/reuse of an implicit representation to rapidly generate realizations.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: struc2vec exhibits much superior performance in capturing stronger notions of structural identity compared to state-of-the-art techniques.
alignments.pub_claim_text: struc2vec exhibits much superior performance in capturing stronger notions of structural identity compared to state-of-the-art techniques.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same performance claim without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Numerical experiments indicate that state-of-the-art techniques for learning node representations fail in capturing stronger notions of structural identity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Numerical experiments indicate that state-of-the-art techniques for learning node representations fail in capturing stronger notions of structural identity.
alignments.rationale: The claim about competing techniques failing to capture structural identity is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: struc2vec improves performance on classification tasks that depend more on structural identity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: struc2vec improves performance on classification tasks that depend more on structural identity.
alignments.rationale: The improvement on structure-dependent classification tasks is stated identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Tensor factorization models can effectively convert massive electronic health records into meaningful clinical concepts for data analysis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Tensor factorization models can effectively convert massive electronic health records into meaningful clinical concepts for data analysis.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same high-level contribution of tensor factorization on EHRs.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed federated tensor factorization method enables computational phenotyping without sharing patient-level data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed solution enables federated tensor factorization for computational phenotyping without sharing patient-level data.
alignments.rationale: Both versions claim the federated method enables phenotyping while preserving patient-level data privacy; wording differs minimally.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method resembles the centralized training model in terms of accuracy and phenotypes discovery while respecting privacy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method resembles the centralized training model in terms of accuracy and phenotypes discovery while respecting privacy.
alignments.rationale: The result comparing federated to centralized training on accuracy and phenotype discovery is identical.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The technological improvements of low-cost small-scale Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) are promoting their increasing use in various tasks.
alignments.rationale: The publication's claims focus on the dataset and do not restate the general background claim about increasing UAV use, so this preprint background claim is absent in the publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Small-scale UAVs are particularly useful for low-altitude tasks that cannot be performed by common UAVs.
alignments.rationale: The publication mentions low-altitude data acquisition but does not make the comparative claim about capabilities versus common UAVs, so the specific preprint claim is not present in the publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The UMCD dataset is the first collection of geo-referenced video sequences acquired at low-altitude for mosaicking and change detection purposes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper provides a UAV mosaicking and change detection dataset consisting of 50 challenging aerial video sequences acquired at low-altitude.
alignments.rationale: The publication still presents the same UMCD dataset (same scope) but omits the preprint's stronger assertion that it is the "first" collection, thereby tempering that aspect of the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Currently, public datasets for testing mosaicking and change detection algorithms at low-altitude are not available.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides such a dataset, which directly contradicts the preprint's statement that no public datasets were available, so this limitation claim is removed/invalidated.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The object-part attention model (OPAM) integrates object-level and part-level attention to enhance multi-view and multi-scale feature learning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The object-part attention model (OPAM) integrates object-level and part-level attention to enhance feature learning for fine-grained image classification.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the integration of object- and part-level attention but frames it explicitly for fine-grained classification, clarifying the application without changing the substance of the contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The object-part spatial constraint model employs both object and part spatial constraints to improve the discrimination of selected parts.
alignments.rationale: The publication's summary claims do not explicitly mention the spatial constraint model; while the model may be in the paper, it is not reflected in the presented publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach does not rely on object or part annotations, thus avoiding the labor-intensive process of labeling.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The OPAM approach does not require object or part annotations, significantly reducing the labor consumption associated with labeling.
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication explicitly state the annotation-free nature of the method and emphasize reduction in labeling effort; the claim is carried over unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The OPAM approach outperforms more than 10 state-of-the-art methods on 4 widely-used datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The OPAM approach achieves the best performance compared to more than ten state-of-the-art methods on four widely-used datasets.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the performance claim verbatim (best performance vs. >10 methods on 4 datasets), so this result claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The D-Wave 2X can be effectively used as part of an unsupervised machine learning method.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The D-Wave 2X can be effectively used as part of an unsupervised machine learning method.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this methodological result directly and without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This method can be used to analyze large datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This method is capable of handling a large input matrix despite the limited number of bits in the D-Wave hardware.
alignments.rationale: The publication refines the broad preprint claim by acknowledging hardware constraints while asserting capability on large input matrices, narrowing and clarifying the original statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The D-Wave only limits the number of features that can be extracted from the dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This method is capable of handling a large input matrix despite the limited number of bits in the D-Wave hardware.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the limitation: rather than stating an absolute limit on feature extraction, it emphasizes that the method can still handle large inputs despite bit limitations, tempering the preprint's stronger limitation claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method is applied to learn the features from a set of facial images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method is able to learn facial features and accurately reproduce the set of facial images.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the facial-features application and strengthens the result by reporting accurate reproduction of the facial images, thus clarifying and amplifying the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Multi-Expert Region-based Convolutional Neural Network (ME R-CNN) better captures the appearance variations of regions of interest (RoIs) caused by different shapes, poses, and viewing angles.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Multi-Expert Region-based Convolutional Neural Network (ME R-CNN) better captures the appearance variations of regions of interest (RoIs) caused by different shapes, poses, and viewing angles.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this core conclusion unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The expert assignment network (EAN) automatically learns the optimal RoI-expert relationship without any supervision of expert assignment.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The expert assignment network (EAN) automatically learns the optimal RoI-expert relationship without any supervision of expert assignment.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this unsupervised assignment claim exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A practical training strategy is introduced to optimize ME, EAN, and the shared network in an end-to-end fashion.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A practical training strategy is introduced to optimize ME, EAN, and the shared network in an end-to-end fashion.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same contribution claim about the training strategy.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Both architectures provide considerable performance increase over the baselines on PASCAL VOC 07, 12, and MS COCO datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Both architectures provide considerable performance increase over the baselines on PASCAL VOC 07, 12, and MS COCO datasets.
alignments.rationale: The reported empirical improvements over baselines are stated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method for automatic optic disc and cup segmentation is based on a modification of the U-Net convolutional neural network.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work presents a universal approach for automatic optic disc and cup segmentation based on a modified U-Net convolutional neural network.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the core methodological contribution (modified U-Net for disc and cup segmentation) directly and consistently.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method achieves quality comparable to current state-of-the-art methods for both optic disc and cup segmentation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method achieves segmentation quality comparable to state-of-the-art methods while outperforming them in prediction time.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the comparable quality claim but adds additional information about prediction-time advantages; the segmentation-quality claim itself is preserved and clarified by the added performance detail.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method outperforms current state-of-the-art methods in terms of prediction time.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method achieves segmentation quality comparable to state-of-the-art methods while outperforming them in prediction time.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly includes the prediction-time outperformance claim verbatim, so this result is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Universal performance bounds are derived for the Bayesian estimation of graph signals from noisy samples.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Universal performance bounds for the Bayesian estimation of graph signals from noisy samples are derived, which are not restricted to specific sampling schemes.
alignments.rationale: The publication integrates the restriction statement into the bound claim; the core result (universal performance bounds) is the same but is presented with additional detail.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The derived performance bounds are not restricted to specific sampling schemes and hold for any sampling sets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Universal performance bounds for the Bayesian estimation of graph signals from noisy samples are derived, which are not restricted to specific sampling schemes.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this restriction claim verbatim as part of Q0; the meaning and scope are preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Near-optimal guarantees for greedy sampling are provided by introducing the concept of approximate submodularity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Near-optimal guarantees for greedy sampling are provided by introducing the concept of approximate submodularity and updating the classical greedy bound.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same core contribution but explicitly notes an update to the classical greedy bound, adding detail to the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Explicit bounds on the approximate supermodularity of the interpolation mean-square error can be optimized with worst-case guarantees using greedy search.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Explicit bounds on the approximate supermodularity of the interpolation mean-square error are shown to be optimizable with worst case guarantees using greedy search.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this technical result with equivalent meaning and scope; wording differences do not change the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Simulations illustrate the derived bound for different graph models and show an application of graph signal sampling to reduce the complexity of kernel principal component analysis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Simulations illustrate the derived bound for different graph models and demonstrate an application of graph signal sampling to reduce the complexity of kernel principal component analysis.
alignments.rationale: The simulation and application claims are duplicated in the publication with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Globally Optimized Dual-Pathway (GoDP) deep architecture outperforms state-of-the-art cascaded regression-based methods on multiple in-the-wild face alignment databases.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Globally Optimized Dual-Pathway (GoDP) deep architecture outperforms state-of-the-art cascaded regression-based methods on multiple in-the-wild face alignment databases.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this performance claim verbatim; there is no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model achieves 1.84 normalized mean error (NME) on the AFLW database, outperforming 3DDFA by 61.8%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model achieves 1.84 normalized mean error (NME) on the AFLW database, outperforming 3DDFA by 61.8%.
alignments.rationale: Numeric performance claim is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments on face identification demonstrate that GoDP, coupled with DPM-headhunter, improves the rank-1 identification rate by 44.2% compared to Dlib toolbox on a challenging database.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments on face identification demonstrate that GoDP, coupled with DPM-headhunter, improves the rank-1 identification rate by 44.2% compared to the Dlib toolbox on a challenging database.
alignments.rationale: The claim is effectively identical; minor article insertion does not change meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach for hand pose estimation demonstrates robustness and accuracy in cluttered real environments using moving egocentric RGB-D cameras.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach for hand pose estimation from moving egocentric RGB-D cameras is robust and accurate in cluttered real environments.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same high-level robustness and accuracy claim with equivalent scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method effectively localizes the hand and regresses 3D joint locations using two Convolutional Neural Networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method demonstrates improved performance in hand-object interactions compared to existing methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts emphasis from a description of the method (localization and regression using two CNNs) to a comparative performance statement on hand-object interactions, changing the nature of the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduction of a new photorealistic dataset enhances the training of the CNNs for natural hand interaction in cluttered scenes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We introduce a new photorealistic dataset that captures and synthesizes large amounts of annotated data for training the CNNs.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the dataset contribution more explicit about scale and synthesis, increasing confidence and detail relative to the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method shows robustness to self-occlusion and occlusions by objects, particularly in moving egocentric perspectives.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method shows robustness to self-occlusion and occlusions by objects, particularly in moving egocentric perspectives.
alignments.rationale: This robustness claim appears verbatim in the publication with equivalent meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed self-adjusting mutation rate in population-based evolutionary algorithms optimizes the OneMax test function more efficiently than the classic $(1+\beta)$ EA.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This time is asymptotically smaller than the optimization time of the classic (1 + ) EA.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the efficiency claim in a precise asymptotic-comparison form; this clarifies the preprint's broader efficiency statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dynamic version of the $(1+\beta)$ EA finds the optimum in an expected optimization time of $O(n\beta/\text{log}\beta+n\text{log} n)$.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dynamic version of the (1 + ) EA finds the optimum in an expected optimization time of O(n/log + n log n).
alignments.rationale: Both statements give the same expected time-bound result (notation differences aside), so the technical claim is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This optimization time is asymptotically smaller than that of the classic $(1+\beta)$ EA.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This time is asymptotically smaller than the optimization time of the classic (1 + ) EA.
alignments.rationale: The asymptotic-comparison conclusion is stated equivalently in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new mutation rate adjustment mechanism is simpler and does not have parameters itself, suggesting potential for other applications.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our adjustment mechanism is simpler than the ones previously used for adjusting the mutation rate and does not have parameters itself.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the simplicity and parameter-free nature of the adjustment mechanism; the formulation is equivalent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning methods improve the performance of recommender systems over traditional methods when review text is available.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning methods improve the performance of recommender systems over traditional methods when review text is available.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this concluding result verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Much of the predictive value of review text comes from reviews of the target user for the target item.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Much of the predictive value of review text comes from reviews of the target user for the target item.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this observation without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TransNets, an extension of the DeepCoNN model, improves substantially over the previous state-of-the-art in recommendation tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: TransNets, an extension of the DeepCoNN model, improves substantially over the previous state-of-the-art in recommendation tasks.
alignments.rationale: The performance-improvement claim is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TransNets introduces an additional latent layer representing the target user-target item pair to enhance recommendation performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: TransNets introduces an additional latent layer representing the target user-target item pair to enhance recommendation performance.
alignments.rationale: The contribution description is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The Augmented-UCB (AugUCB) algorithm is the first to use both mean and variance estimates for the thresholding bandit problem. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An upper bound on the loss incurred by AugUCB has been theoretically obtained. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AugUCB does not require access to problem complexity inputs, making it more practical than UCBEV. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Unlike UCBEV, AugUCB does not require access to problem complexity inputs, making it more practical for real-world applications. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Simulation experiments demonstrate that AugUCB performs significantly better than state-of-the-art algorithms like APT and CSAR. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Simulation experiments demonstrate that AugUCB performs significantly better than state-of-the-art algorithms such as APT and CSAR. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental performance claim is carried over intact and matches the same baselines and strength of result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed lifelong learning solution effectively preserves knowledge of previous tasks while learning new ones using autoencoders. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed lifelong learning solution effectively preserves knowledge of previous tasks while learning new ones using autoencoders. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim about preserving prior knowledge using autoencoders with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method prevents the reconstructions of features from changing, which helps maintain performance on previously learned tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method prevents the reconstructions of features from changing, which helps maintain performance on previously learned tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result about stabilized reconstructions and maintained performance is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed system demonstrates a reduction of forgetting compared to the state-of-the-art in image classification tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed system demonstrates a reduction of forgetting compared to the state-of-the-art in image classification tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication carries over the experimental claim of reduced forgetting against state-of-the-art with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work proposes an online hash model to accommodate data coming in stream for online learning. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This paper proposes an online hash model to accommodate data coming in stream for online learning. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is the same in both versions, matching intent and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new loss function is proposed to measure the similarity loss between a pair of data samples in hamming space. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A new loss function is proposed to measure the similarity loss between a pair of data samples in hamming space. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The proposed loss function claim is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Theoretical analysis on the upper bound of the cumulative loss for the proposed online hash model is provided. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Theoretical analysis on the upper bound of the cumulative loss for the proposed online hash model is provided. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Theoretical upper-bound analysis is present in both versions with equivalent framing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The competitive efficiency and effectiveness of the proposed online hash models are verified through extensive experiments on several large-scale datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The competitive efficiency and effectiveness of the proposed online hash models are verified through extensive experiments on several large-scale data sets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Experimental verification claim matches between preprint and publication with only trivial wording changes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The online hashing is extended from a single-model to a multi-model online hashing to retain diverse online hashing models. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The online hashing (OH) is extended from a single model to a multimodel OH to retain diverse OH models and avoid biased update. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication elaborates (adds rationale 'avoid biased update') but preserves the core extension to multimodel online hashing; this is a clarification rather than a substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Non-consumption by a user does not always mean disinterest or irrelevance. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Non-consumption by a user does not always mean disinterest or irrelevance. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about non-consumption being ambiguous is carried over verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method, Conformative Filtering (CoF), addresses the issue of negative examples in implicit feedback for item recommendation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method, Conformative Filtering (CoF), addresses the issue of negative examples in implicit feedback for item recommendation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim introducing CoF and its intended issue is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CoF performs multidimensional clustering on implicit feedback data to identify user 'tastes' groups. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CoF performs multidimensional clustering on implicit feedback data to identify user 'taste' groups. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The method-description is the same, but the publication increases the stated certainty of this result from medium to high, so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments show that CoF has superior performance compared to several common baselines. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments show that CoF has superior performance compared to several common baselines. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental superiority claim is preserved verbatim with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Human judgments about preferences in decision making are very rarely consistent, leading to significant variations in the quality of priority ratios estimation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Human judgments are consistently inconsistent, leading to significant variations in preference estimation quality. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Wording changed but the substantive claim—that human judgments are inconsistent and that causes variation in estimation quality—is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The scale of variations in priority ratios estimation depends on the applied prioritization procedures and utilized consistency measures for a pairwise comparison matrix. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The scale of variations in preference estimation quality depends on the chosen consistency measure of pairwise judgments. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication narrows the dependency claim to focus on the chosen consistency measure (removing explicit mention of prioritization procedures), clarifying and narrowing the original scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Commonly applied prioritization procedures and consistency measures in the Analytic Hierarchy Process may significantly deteriorate the quality of priority ratios estimation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There are consistency measures whose performance may confuse decision makers regarding the quality of their ranking outcome. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts from a broad claim that commonly applied procedures/measures may deteriorate estimation to a narrower claim that some consistency measures can confuse decision makers, which is a revision and somewhat softened framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Proposed solutions in this paper can significantly improve the methodology for estimating priority ratios. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The solutions proposed in this paper can significantly improve the quality of preference estimation. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the benefit of the proposed solutions with slightly different emphasis (quality of estimation vs methodology) but retains the same intent and strength.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper theoretically proves that with high probability the affinity or distance between two projected subspaces are concentrated around their estimates. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper theoretically proves that with high probability the affinity or distance between two projected subspaces are concentrated around their estimates. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim is verbatim the same as the preprint, so the meaning, scope, and confidence are unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: When the ambient dimension after projection is sufficiently large, the affinity and distance between two subspaces almost remain unchanged after random projection. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: When the ambient dimension after projection is sufficiently large, the affinity and distance between two subspaces almost remain unchanged after random projection. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This claim appears identical in wording and strength between preprint and publication, so no substantive change is detected.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Numerical experiments verify the theoretical work presented in the paper. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Numerical experiments verify the theoretical work presented in the paper. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental-verification claim is carried over verbatim to the publication with identical scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The hardware shows 97.5 percent accuracy in classification, which is equivalent to software for a Fisher-Iris dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The hardware shows 97.5% accuracy in classification, which is equivalent to software for a Fisher's Iris dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numeric result and claim of equivalence to software on the Iris dataset are preserved with only minor typographic wording changes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Learning and recognition occur in separate arrays simultaneously in real-time, avoiding non-biomimetic clocking based complex signal management. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The learning and recognition occurs in separate arrays simultaneously in real-time, asynchronously, avoiding non-biomimetic clocking based complex signal management. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the descriptor 'asynchronously', clarifying the operational mode; this refines wording without changing the claim's scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An increasingly realistic memristor model slightly reduces the hardware performance to 85 percent, highlighting the need to engineer RRAM characteristics specifically for SNN. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An increasingly realistic memristor model slightly reduces the hardware performance to 85%, highlighting the need to engineer RRAM characteristics specifically for SNN. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation and quantification are the same in preprint and publication; only minor formatting differences are present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deterministic quantum annealing expectation-maximization (DQAEM) algorithm is proposed as a quantum annealing extension of the expectation-maximization (EM) algorithm. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deterministic quantum annealing expectation-maximization (DQAEM) algorithm is proposed as a quantum annealing extension of the expectation-maximization (EM) algorithm. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The description of the proposed algorithm is identical between preprint and publication, so it is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DQAEM outperforms EM in maximum likelihood estimation (MLE) as demonstrated by numerical simulations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Numerical simulations illustrate that DQAEM moderates the problem of local optima in the EM algorithm. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint asserted outright outperforming in MLE, while the publication presents a more specific result about moderating local optima; this is a narrowing and softening of the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DQAEM algorithm has advantages in terms of the path integral formulation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DQAEM demonstrates advantages in terms of the path integral formulation compared to traditional EM. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the path-integral advantage more explicitly and at higher certainty, clarifying and strengthening the earlier, lower-confidence conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model consists of a deep CNN shaped like a tree that enables end-to-end learning of separate visual features for different classes. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model consists of a deep CNN shaped like a tree that enables end-to-end learning of separate visual features for different classes. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The core contribution claim is carried over verbatim from preprint to publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our resulting model yields a substantial improvement in accuracy over the original CNN on the CIFAR100, CIFAR10, and Synth datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our scheme acts as a form of beneficial regularization, improving generalization performance. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed scheme acts as a form of beneficial regularization, improving generalization performance. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The user-driven intelligent interface for accessibility is proposed based on multimodal augmented reality and brain-computer interaction. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Brain-computer interface (BCI) technology provides new strategies to overcome limits of currently available user interfaces, especially for people with functional disabilities. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The combination of machine learning, multimodal interactions, and BCI will benefit from immediate feedback from actual neurophysical reactions classified by machine learning methods. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Combined AR-BCI interfaces could provide highly adaptable and personal services, especially for people with functional disabilities. (certainty=medium, type=result)
