alignments.preprint_claim_text: Each positive global minimum of the associated functional is a solution to the problem.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Each positive global minimum of the associated functional corresponds to a solution of the problem.
alignments.rationale: The statement is effectively the same, but the publication lists a lower certainty level, so semantically unchanged but confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Numerical results demonstrate a better performance of the proposed method compared to previous TV based models for rician denoising.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method demonstrates better performance compared to previous TV-based models for Rician denoising.
alignments.rationale: The numerical-performance claim is preserved verbatim between preprint and publication with similar certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An application on real Diffusion Tensor Images affected by rician noise is presented and discussed.
alignments.rationale: No corresponding application-on-real-DTI claim appears in the publication; the paper instead adds algorithmic solution details not present in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The Proximal Point Algorithm effectively solves the nonsmooth nonconvex minimization problem associated with Rician denoising.
alignments.rationale: This algorithmic/implementation claim appears in the publication but was not present in the preprint, so it is an added contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed appearance modeling approach provides accurate appearance affinities to guide data association in online multi-person tracking.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed appearance modeling approach provides accurate appearance affinities to guide data association in online multi-person tracking.
alignments.rationale: Claim language and meaning are preserved between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Exploiting the temporal dynamic characteristics within temporal appearance sequences significantly improves the affinity measurement between trajectories and detections.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Exploiting the temporal dynamic characteristics within temporal appearance sequences significantly improves the affinity measurement between trajectories and detections.
alignments.rationale: Direct correspondence with identical statement and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The feature selection algorithm effectively describes appearance variations with mid-level semantic features.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The feature selection algorithm effectively describes appearance variations with mid-level semantic features.
alignments.rationale: Same claim present in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The learned appearance model allows for reliable tracking of multiple persons in complex scenes when incorporated into an online tracking-by-detection framework.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Reliable tracking of multiple persons in complex scenes is achieved by incorporating the learned model into an online tracking-by-detection framework.
alignments.rationale: Equivalent conclusion retained in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments on the benchmark MOTChallenge 2015 demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms state-of-the-art multi-person tracking algorithms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms the state-of-the-art multi-person tracking algorithms.
alignments.rationale: Experimental superiority claim is preserved with comparable wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed framework detects traffic signs, cars, and cyclists in a single learning-based detection framework.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed framework detects traffic signs, cars, and cyclists in a single learning-based detection framework.
alignments.rationale: Direct match between preprint and publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using a common framework for detection significantly increases detection speed by evaluating dense features only once during the testing phase.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The detection speed of the proposed framework is much faster because all dense features need only to be evaluated once in the testing phase.
alignments.rationale: Same speed-related claim kept unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduction of spatially pooled features enhances feature robustness to noises and image deformations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The introduction of spatially pooled features enhances feature robustness to noises and image deformations.
alignments.rationale: Claim about spatially pooled features is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The object subcategorization method improves the generalization performance by capturing intra-class variation of objects.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed object subcategorization method improves the generalization performance by capturing the intraclass variation of objects.
alignments.rationale: Subcategorization claim retained with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed framework achieves competitive performance compared to state-of-the-art approaches on several benchmark datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed framework achieves competitive performance with state-of-the-art approaches on several benchmark data sets.
alignments.rationale: Performance claim matches the publication wording and intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: High frequency oscillations (HFOs) are a promising biomarker of epileptic brain tissue and activity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: High frequency oscillations (HFOs) are a promising biomarker of epileptic brain tissue and activity.
alignments.rationale: The biomarker conclusion is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Previous HFO analyses have assumed a linear manifold that is global across time, space, and individual patients.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Previous HFO analysis has assumed a linear manifold that is global across time, space, and individual patients.
alignments.rationale: The limitation about prior linear-manifold assumptions is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This analysis assesses the appropriateness of linear methods and the consistency of the manifold across time, space, and patients.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study assesses the appropriateness of linear methods for analyzing HFOs.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the assessment of linear methods but drops the explicit claim about manifold consistency across time/space/patients, so the claim is clarified and narrowed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study estimates bounds on the Bayes classification error to quantify the distinction between two classes of HFOs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This analysis estimates bounds on the Bayes classification error to quantify the distinction between two classes of HFOs.
alignments.rationale: The Bayes-error-bounding analysis is present with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analysis provides a foundation for future clinical use of HFO features and builds the analysis for other discrete events.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings provide a foundation for future clinical use of HFO features and guide the analysis of other discrete events.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the future-clinical-foundation claim but states it with higher certainty, so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: t-SNE provides better, more interpretable results than PCA for visualizing Polish case law.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The t-Distributed Stochastic Neighbor Embedding method (t-SNE) is effectively applied to visualize judgments from Polish courts.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps that t-SNE is effective for visualizing court judgments but drops the explicit comparative claim against PCA, so the claim is revised and narrowed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: t-SNE reveals hidden topical structure of documents related to the keyword 'pension'.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The visualizations obtained from the t-SNE maps are encouraging.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's specific claim about revealing pension-related topical structure is softened in the publication to a more general, weaker assessment of the visualizations as encouraging.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The t-SNE method could be a promising tool to facilitate the exploratory analysis of legal texts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The generated t-SNE maps are interpretable and capable of providing synthetic knowledge about document collections.
alignments.rationale: The publication concretely states that t-SNE maps are interpretable and provide synthetic knowledge, amplifying the preprint's tentative "could be promising" wording.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The t-SNE dimensionality reduction technique allows for the mapping of each document onto a point in two-dimensional space based on vocabulary.
alignments.rationale: This methodological description of how t-SNE maps documents (2D mapping based on vocabulary) appears in the publication but is not present in the preprint, so it is an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Text-Attentional Convolutional Neural Network (Text-CNN) effectively extracts text-related regions and features from image components. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include an explicit claim that the Text-CNN 'extracts text-related regions and features'; related publication claims focus on discrimination and robustness rather than explicit feature/region extraction.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The rich supervision information used in training the Text-CNN enhances its capability for discriminating ambiguous texts and increases robustness against complicated background components. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Text-CNN demonstrates a strong capability for discriminating ambiguous texts and increases robustness against complicated background components. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same capability and robustness claim with essentially the same emphasis and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The training process formulated as a multi-task learning problem significantly facilitates the main task of text/non-text classification. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The training process of the Text-CNN is formulated as a multi-task learning problem, which facilitates the main task of text/non-text classification. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same multi-task learning formulation and its facilitation of text/non-text classification with equivalent framing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Contrast-Enhancement Maximally Stable Extremal Regions (CE-MSERs) detector improves the detection of challenging text patterns, resulting in a higher recall. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The contrast-enhancement maximally stable extremal regions (MSERs) detector enhances intensity contrast between text patterns and background, allowing it to detect highly challenging text patterns. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the mechanism (enhancing intensity contrast) by which CE-MSERs detect challenging text; this clarifies the preprint's claim about improved detection/recall without changing scope or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach achieved a F-measure of 0.82 on the ICDAR 2013 dataset, substantially improving state-of-the-art results. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach achieved an F-measure of 0.82 on the ICDAR 2013 data set, substantially improving the state-of-the-art results. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The F-measure result is reported identically in the publication with the same characterization relative to state-of-the-art.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Numenta Anomaly Benchmark (NAB) provides a controlled and repeatable environment to test and measure anomaly detection algorithms on streaming data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Numenta Anomaly Benchmark (NAB) provides a controlled and repeatable environment to test and measure anomaly detection algorithms on streaming data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: NAB formalizes the characteristics of an ideal anomaly detector, which includes detecting all anomalies promptly, triggering no false alarms, and adapting to changing statistics. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: NAB formalizes the characteristics of an ideal anomaly detector, including detecting all anomalies promptly, triggering no false alarms, and adapting to changing statistics. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same formalization of ideal detector characteristics with matching detail and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: NAB evaluates detectors using a benchmark dataset that consists of labeled, real-world time-series data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: NAB evaluates detectors using a benchmark dataset with labeled, real-world time-series data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The dataset description and evaluation purpose are the same in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The goal of NAB is to provide a standard, open source framework for the research community to compare and evaluate different algorithms for detecting anomalies in streaming data. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The goal of NAB is to provide a standard, open source framework for the research community to compare and evaluate different algorithms for detecting anomalies in streaming data. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The stated goal is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method for learning high-order MGRF models is effective for a broad selection of complex textures. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method for learning high-order MGRF models effectively synthesizes a broad selection of complex textures. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Learning interaction structure significantly benefits the modeling of textures with large-scale structure. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The texture models were found to be competitive with other works in grading synthesised textures and in a constrained inpainting task. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The texture models were found to be competitive with other works in grading synthesized textures and in a constrained inpainting task. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Taking pictures of food in restaurants can be used for automating food journaling. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed system leverages restaurant context and online information to enhance food recognition. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of the food recognition system was evaluated in real-world settings across 10 restaurants and 5 different types of food. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The technique uses images, videos, and sensor data from first-person point-of-view devices for egocentric field-of-view localization. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The technique uses images, videos, and sensor data from first-person point-of-view devices for egocentric field-of-view localization. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Egocentric FOV localization captures visual information from a person's field-of-view and transfers it onto a reference corpus of images and videos. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method matches first-person perspective images and videos with a reference corpus and refines results using head orientation information from device sensors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method matches first-person perspective images and videos with a reference corpus and refines results using head orientation information from device sensors. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The technique demonstrates single and multi-user egocentric FOV localization in various indoor and outdoor environments. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The technique demonstrates single and multi-user egocentric FOV localization in various indoor and outdoor environments. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Demonstration claims are preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The applications of this technique include augmented reality, event understanding, and studying social interactions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Applications of the technique include augmented reality, event understanding, and studying social interactions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A Universal Differential Evolution (UDE) is proposed that utilizes both design and fitness spaces criteria for intelligent selection of mutation vectors. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Union Differential Evolution (UDE) is proposed to enhance the intelligent selection of mutation vectors by utilizing both design and fitness spaces criteria. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The experimental analysis on UDE demonstrates that it significantly improves the performance of differential evolution compared to methods that use only one criterion for intelligent selection. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: UDE significantly improved the performance of Differential Evolution (DE) compared to methods that only use one criterion for intelligent selection. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Previous methods for intelligent selection of mutation vectors in Differential Evolution disregarded half of the valuable information of the problem space. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Previous methods for intelligent selection of mutation vectors in DE disregarded half of the valuable information of the problem space. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation statement is carried over exactly in meaning and emphasis between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Earth Mover's Intersection (EMI) is a positive definite analog to Earth Mover's Distance (EMD) for sets of different sizes. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We develop a set-theoretic interpretation of the earth mover's distance (EMD) and propose earth mover's intersection (EMI), a PD analog to EMD for sets of different sizes. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution (EMI as a PD analog to EMD) with additional framing; the technical claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Conditions are provided under which EMD or certain approximations to EMD are negative definite. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We provide conditions under which EMD or certain approximations to EMD are negative definite. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication carries the same technical statement about conditions for negative definiteness with equivalent emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A positive-definite-preserving transformation can be applied to any kernel to derive positive definite EMD-based kernels. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We present a PD-preserving transformation that can be applied to any kernel and can also be used to derive PD EMD-based kernels. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the contribution of a PD-preserving transformation with the same applicability and implications.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Jaccard index is the result of the positive-definite-preserving transformation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Jaccard index is simply the result of this transformation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both versions assert that the Jaccard index arises from the transformation; wording is effectively identical in meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: EMD is generally inferior to the proposed EMI and transformation in various computer vision tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We show that EMD is generally inferior even with indefinite kernel techniques when evaluated against kernels based on EMI and the proposed transformation in various computer vision tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens and clarifies the empirical claim by adding that EMD remains inferior even when using indefinite-kernel techniques, broadening the comparison and slightly amplifying the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper demonstrates the approximation hardness of inner product similarity join (IPS join) in subquadratic time, assuming the strong exponential time hypothesis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper demonstrates the approximation hardness of inner product similarity join in subquadratic time, assuming the strong exponential time hypothesis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The hardness result is stated identically in the publication; the assumption and claim remain the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: New upper and lower bounds for (A)LSH-based algorithms are established, showing that asymmetry can be avoided by relaxing the LSH definition to only consider the collision probability of distinct elements. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: New upper and lower bounds for (A)LSH-based algorithms are established. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication splits this combined preprint claim into separate publication claims (bounds vs. the asymmetry observation); mapping to the bounds claim narrows the original combined statement while preserving the bounds result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new indexing method for IPS based on linear sketches is introduced, implying that the hardness results are not far from being tight. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A new indexing method for IPS based on linear sketches is introduced, implying that the hardness results are not far from being tight. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same methodological contribution but reduces the stated certainty, so the content is unchanged but confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper provides greater clarity on the inner product similarity join by distinguishing among signed and unsigned variants and shedding new light on the effect of asymmetry. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper provides greater clarity by distinguishing among signed and unsigned variants of IPS join and shedding new light on the effect of asymmetry. (certainty=low, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same conceptual contribution but lowers the expressed certainty, so the claim is effectively the same but with tempered confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel feature in the form of spectrum images of vibration signals is presented for bearing fault diagnosis. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel feature in the form of images, specifically spectrum images of vibration signals, is presented for bearing fault diagnosis. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same contribution (spectrum-image feature) with equivalent emphasis and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The spectrum images are obtained by performing fast Fourier transformation on vibration signals. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The spectrum images are obtained by performing fast Fourier transformation on vibration signals. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Method of obtaining the spectrum images is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Two-dimensional principal component analysis (2DPCA) is used to reduce the dimensions of the spectrum images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Two-dimensional principal component analysis (2DPCA) is used to reduce the dimensions of the spectrum images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The dimensionality-reduction step is unchanged in content and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A minimum distance method is applied to classify the faults of bearings using the processed images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A minimum distance method is applied to classify the faults of bearings based on the processed images. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The effectiveness of the proposed method is verified with experimental data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The effectiveness of the proposed method for bearing fault diagnosis is verified with experimental data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental validation claim is carried forward without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The information contained in measurements can be sufficient for satisfactory inference under certain conditions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The information contained in measurements can be sufficient for satisfactory inference under certain conditions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Understanding fundamental barriers in inference problems can be approached by considering them as phase transitions in statistical physics. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: New promising algorithms can be developed using the physical insights gained from the connection between inference and statistical physics. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: New promising algorithms can be developed using insights gained from the connection between inference and statistical physics. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The registration of in-vivo Drosophila brain volumes visualizing genetically encoded calcium indicators to an nc82 atlas is ineffective with traditional cost functions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The registration of in-vivo Drosophila brain volumes visualizing genetically encoded calcium indicators to an nc82 atlas is ineffective with traditional cost functions.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same negative finding about traditional cost functions for this registration task.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning based contrast synthesis can yield improvements in multi-modal image registration.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning based contrast synthesis can yield improvements in multi-modal image registration.
alignments.rationale: Both describe the same conclusion that learned contrast synthesis improves multimodal registration.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The number of subjects for which the registration outright failed was greatly reduced from 40% to 15% by using a synthesized image.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The number of subjects for which the registration outright failed was greatly reduced from 40% to 15% by using a synthesized image.
alignments.rationale: The quantitative reduction in outright failures is presented identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Bayesian Rule Lists has predictive accuracy on par with the current top algorithms for prediction in machine learning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Bayesian Rule Lists has predictive accuracy on par with the current top algorithms for prediction in machine learning.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same claim about predictive accuracy relative to top algorithms.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Bayesian Rule Lists can be used to produce highly accurate and interpretable medical scoring systems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method can be used to produce highly accurate and interpretable medical scoring systems.
alignments.rationale: Both claims state the same application to medical scoring systems; publication wording is slightly more generic but meaning is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model produced is as interpretable as CHADS$_2$, but more accurate.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our model is as interpretable as CHADS$_{2}$, but more accurate.
alignments.rationale: The interpretability and improved accuracy relative to CHADS2 is stated equivalently in publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Bayesian Rule Lists employs a novel prior structure to encourage sparsity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Bayesian Rule Lists employs a novel prior structure to encourage sparsity.
alignments.rationale: The contribution about the prior structure and sparsity encouragement is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduced algorithm transforms any set of graded attribute implications into an equivalent non-redundant set with saturated consequents. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The introduced algorithm transforms any set of graded attribute implications into an equivalent non-redundant set with saturated consequents. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication statements are textually identical and convey the same result without modification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper solves the open problem regarding the existence of general systems of pseudo-intents in formal concept analysis of object-attribute data. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper solves the open problem regarding the existence of general systems of pseudo-intents in formal concept analysis of object-attribute data. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both versions assert the same conclusion with identical wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A polynomial-time procedure is shown for determining bases given by general systems of pseudo-intents from complete sets of graded attribute implications. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A polynomial-time procedure is shown for determining bases given by general systems of pseudo-intents from complete sets of graded attribute implications. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is presented identically in the publication, with no change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The methods developed for recognizing named entities in literary Chinese performed very well on practical evaluations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our methods performed very well on realistic tests conducted with texts extracted from more than 220 volumes of local gazetteers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication gives a more specific description of the evaluations (realistic tests, >220 volumes), which clarifies but does not change the claim's intent or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Thousands of names and addresses were identified from the texts of local gazetteers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Thousands of names and addresses were identified from the texts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result (identification of thousands of names/addresses) with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A good portion of the extracted names matches the biographical information currently recorded in the China Biographical Database (CBDB). (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A good portion of the extracted names match the biographical information currently recorded in the China Biographical Database (CBDB) of Harvard University. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the institutional identifier for the CBDB, clarifying the reference without altering the substantive claim or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Many of the extracted names can be verified by historians and will become new additions to the CBDB. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Many other extracted names can be verified by historians and will become new additions to the CBDB. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication makes a stylistic tweak ('many other') but retains the same conclusion regarding verifiability and addition to CBDB.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed estimation method accurately recovers the parameters of a d-dimensional Hawkes point-process from a set of observations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed estimation method accurately recovers the parameters of a d-dimensional Hawkes point-process from a set of observations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is stated identically in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mean-field approximation is valid when the fluctuations of the stochastic intensity are small. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The mean-field approximation is valid when the fluctuations of the stochastic intensity are small, particularly in cases of weak interactions, high system dimensions, or self-averaging fluctuations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds specific typical scenarios (clarification) and presents the claim with lower stated certainty (tempered confidence).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed estimator, while biased, has precision comparable to the Maximum Likelihood Estimator and significantly improved computation speed. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed estimator, while biased, achieves precision comparable to that of the maximum likelihood estimator and significantly improves computation speed. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same comparison of precision and computational improvement without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The accuracy of the new approach is theoretically controlled and illustrated using synthetic datasets. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The accuracy of the new approach is theoretically controlled and illustrated using synthetic datasets to assess the statistical estimation error of the parameters. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies the purpose of the synthetic experiments (assessing statistical estimation error) while preserving the original claim and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method of using low-cost gyroscopes to regularize the tracking energy function significantly improves performance over conventional template-based tracking methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method of using low-cost gyroscopes to regularize the tracking energy function significantly improves performance over conventional template-based tracking methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is carried over verbatim and unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our technique is competitive with more complex and computationally expensive state-of-the-art trackers while operating at a fraction of the computational cost. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our technique is competitive with more complex and computationally expensive state-of-the-art trackers while operating at a fraction of the computational cost. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same performance and computational-cost claim without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Initializing template-based feature trackers like KLT using gyro-predicted optical flow offers no advantage over a careful optical-only initialization method. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Initializing template-based feature trackers like KLT using gyro-predicted optical flow offers no advantage over careful optical-only initialization methods. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about initialization remains the same in publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A deeper level of integration, like the method we propose, is needed to realize a genuine improvement in tracking performance from inertial sensors. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A deeper level of integration, as proposed in our method, is needed to realize genuine improvements in tracking performance from inertial sensors. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same conclusion advocating deeper integration of inertial sensors for performance gains.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new CNN-based filter effectively removes non-informative images from egocentric photo streams. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A new CNN-based filter effectively removes non-informative images from egocentric photo streams. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The core result about the CNN filter is stated identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Images are ranked by relevance to ensure semantic diversity and re-ranked by a novelty criterion to reduce redundancy. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Images are ranked by relevance to ensure semantic diversity and re-ranked by a novelty criterion to reduce redundancy. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same methodological claim but increases the stated certainty, reflecting amplified confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new evaluation metric is proposed that accounts for the non-uniqueness of the solution in image summarization. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A new evaluation metric is proposed that accounts for the non-uniqueness of the solution in image summarization. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same contribution but reports lower certainty for this claim, so the confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The experimental results show 95.74% expert satisfaction and a Mean Opinion Score of 4.57 out of 5.0. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results show 95.74% satisfaction from experts and a Mean Opinion Score of 4.57 out of 5.0. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numerical experimental results are identical and unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We propose the first method for spotting spontaneous micro-expressions in long videos, which is training free and works on arbitrary unseen videos. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose the first method for spotting spontaneous micro-expressions in long videos, which is training free and works on arbitrary unseen videos. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Text is effectively identical between preprint and publication, same claim and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We present an advanced micro-expression recognition framework that outperforms previous work by a large margin on two challenging spontaneous micro-expression databases. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We present an advanced micro-expression recognition framework that outperforms previous work by a large margin on two challenging spontaneous micro-expression databases (SMIC and CASMEII). (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Publication adds the specific dataset names (SMIC and CASMEII) but the substantive performance claim is the same, so this is a clarification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We propose the first automatic micro-expression analysis system (MESR) that can spot and recognize micro-expressions from spontaneous video data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose the first automatic micro-expression analysis system (MESR) that can spot and recognize micro-expressions from spontaneous video data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The claim appears verbatim and unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method outperforms humans in the micro-expression recognition task by a large margin and achieves comparable performance to humans in spotting and recognizing spontaneous micro-expressions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method outperforms humans in the micro-expression recognition task by a large margin and achieves comparable performance to humans in the task of spotting and then recognizing spontaneous micro-expressions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication rewords the spotting+recognition formulation slightly but conveys the same result; wording is marginally clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Evolving fitness distributions are attracted to a one-parameter family of distributions with a fixed parabolic relationship between skewness and kurtosis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The tail indices of fitness distributions control their late-time evolution. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint's specific one-parameter/shape relation is reframed in the publication in terms of tail indices controlling late-time behavior; this is a conceptual clarification linking the earlier shape claim to tail-index control.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mean fitness of a population under the selection of pre-existing variation is a power-law function of time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The location, scale, and shape of the fitness distribution can be predicted far into the future from the measurement of the tail index at some initial time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint's specific power-law-in-time statement is not echoed verbatim; the publication emphasizes predictability from tail index (location/scale/shape) which revises the form of the temporal prediction.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our results resolve the 'dynamic insufficiency' of Fisher's fundamental theorem of natural selection. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The identified pattern of fitness distribution evolution encompasses both positive and negative selection and is not restricted to rapidly adapting populations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The strong preprint conclusion about resolving dynamic insufficiency is softened in the publication to a broader descriptive result about patterns of evolution; the claim is therefore revised and somewhat tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our predictions are in good agreement with numerical simulations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results are well supported by numerical simulations from both the Wright-Fisher model and a less structured genetic algorithm. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both statements assert strong agreement with numerical simulations; the publication gives more detail about simulation types but the substantive claim is unchanged.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Fisher's fundamental theorem of natural selection states that the instantaneous rate of growth of a population's mean fitness is its variance in fitness. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication includes an explicit statement of Fisher's theorem as background/ framing that was not listed as an explicit claim in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ProtNN is a novel approach for protein function prediction that utilizes protein-graph pairwise similarities. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication focuses on performance and scalability results and does not explicitly restate the preprint's novelty/contribution phrasing about protein-graph pairwise similarities, so the explicit contribution claim is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental evaluation demonstrates that ProtNN is able to accurately classify several datasets in an extremely fast runtime compared to state-of-the-art approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ProtNN is able to accurately classify several datasets in an extremely fast runtime compared to state-of-the-art approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental performance and fast runtime claim appears verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ProtNN is able to scale up to a whole PDB dataset in a single-process mode with a gain of thousands order of magnitude of runtime compared to state-of-the-art approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ProtNN is able to scale up to a whole PDB dataset in a single-process mode with no parallelization. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes single-process scalability; the preprint combined that scalability claim with a quantified runtime gain (split across publication claims), so mapping to the scaling statement is a clarification of the scalability aspect.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: For buyers with Matroid Based Valuations, overdemand with respect to equilibrium prices is at most 1, independent of the supply of goods, even when tie-breaking is done in an uncoordinated fashion. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Under a mild genericity condition, the over-demand of any good can be bounded by 1 at the minimal prices. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces a mild genericity condition and frames the result at minimal prices; this refines/clarifies the preprint's unconditional statement, effectively narrowing its scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Prices are robust to changing the buyers in the market, so long as all buyers are sampled from the same (unknown) distribution. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The exact Walrasian equilibrium prices computed in a market induce both low over-demand and high welfare in a new market with independently sampled agents. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication formalizes robustness via a statement about equilibrium prices transferring low over-demand and high welfare to a new market with independently sampled agents, clarifying and supporting the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Prices alone are not sufficient to coordinate the market, and buyers may need to select among their most preferred bundles in a coordinated way to find a feasible allocation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The minimal Walrasian equilibrium prices induce allocations that result in low over-demand, regardless of how buyers break ties. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint's caution that prices alone are insufficient is contradicted by the publication's stronger claim that minimal equilibrium prices induce low over-demand regardless of tie-breaking; this represents a substantive revision and a tempering of the preprint conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed band selection strategy in reproducing kernel Hilbert spaces drastically reduces the processing time required by nonlinear unmixing techniques. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed band selection strategy in reproducing kernel Hilbert spaces drastically reduces the processing time required by nonlinear unmixing techniques. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim appears verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Simulation results demonstrate a complexity reduction of two orders of magnitude. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Simulation results demonstrate a complexity reduction of two orders of magnitude. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numerical complexity reduction claim is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method does not compromise unmixing performance. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method does not compromise unmixing performance. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The performance-preserving conclusion is stated identically in the publication.
